content.content:
# Masonry Layout Status and Browser Support

## Baseline Status
The page clearly states that masonry layout has **"Limited availability"** and is **"not Baseline because it does not work in some of the most widely-used browsers."**

## Experimental Status
Masonry layout is marked as **experimental technology**. The page includes this warning:
> **Experimental:** **This is an [experimental technology](/en-US/docs/MDN/Writing_guidelines/Experimental_deprecated_obsolete#experimental)**  
> Check the [Browser compatibility table](#browser_compatibility) carefully before using this in production.

## Key Warnings
The documentation strongly advises:
- ⚠️ **Do not use in production** without checking browser compatibility
- Users are encouraged to tell the MDN team why they need this feature via the web platform feedback system

## Implementation
Despite the limited support, the feature is defined in **Level 3 of the CSS Grid Layout specification** and is accessed via:
- `display: grid-lanes` 
- `display: inline-grid-lanes`

## Practical Guidance
The page notes that **in browsers that do not support masonry layout**, regular grid auto-placement will be used as a fallback:
> In browsers [that do not support masonry](#browser_compatibility), regular grid auto-placement will be used instead.

## Summary
As of 2025-2026, masonry layout remains an experimental feature with limited browser support across widely-used browsers, making it unsuitable for production use at this time despite being a useful layout method for tightly-packed, Pinterest-style layouts.
